registry.description: Terror is the most extreme form of fear — the inner state produced by a threat so overwhelming that it shatters the capacity for rational response. The Hebrew vocabulary here is exceptionally rich: terror, trembling, dismay, shuddering, and the particular phrase terror on every side that appears in Jeremiah. Terror has a theological dimension: the Day of the Lord is described in terror vocabulary, and the enemies of God's people are struck with terror before them. It is the appropriate response of the creature to unconstrained power.

terms.medium_def:
: frightening(DANGER)
1) to fear, revere, be afraid
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to fear, be afraid
1a2) to stand in awe of, be awed
1a3) to fear, reverence, honour, respect
1b) (Niphal)
1b1) to be fearful, be dreadful, be feared
1b2) to cause astonishment and awe, be held in awe
1b3) to inspire reverence or godly fear or awe
1c) (Piel) to make afraid, terrify
2) (TWOT) to shoot, pour

terms.medium_def:
: awesome(god)
1) to fear, revere, be afraid
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to fear, be afraid
1a2) to stand in awe of, be awed
1a3) to fear, reverence, honour, respect
1b) (Niphal)
1b1) to be fearful, be dreadful, be feared
1b2) to cause astonishment and awe, be held in awe
1b3) to inspire reverence or godly fear or awe
1c) (Piel) to make afraid, terrify
2) (TWOT) to shoot, pour
Also means: ya.re (יָרֵא ": frightening" H3372)
